codeReview.summary: Implementation is clean and well-structured. The HttpError base class provides a solid foundation, and all four error classes (NotFoundError, ConflictError, ValidationError, UnauthorizedError) are correctly implemented with appropriate status codes. Tests are comprehensive with 27 test cases covering all assertions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The error class pattern uses a base HttpError class that extends native Error with a readonly statusCode property. The name property is correctly set via this.constructor.name in the base class constructor, ensuring all subclasses automatically get their correct class name.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: server/errors.ts:5-12 — HttpError base class pattern followed by all four subclasses

sharedStateObservations.observation: Error classes use default message parameters in constructors, providing sensible defaults while allowing custom messages for specific cases.

summary: PASS: Backend error classes implementation is complete and correct. All five assertions (VAL-ERR-009, VAL-ERR-010, VAL-ERR-011, VAL-ERR-012, VAL-ERR-026) are fully satisfied. The HttpError base class design is extensible for future error types. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
